content_blocks.name: Ch 3: Medical, Legal, and Ethical Issues
content_blocks.notes: High discussion value — allow extra time

content_blocks.name: Ch 6: Lifting & Moving Patients
content_blocks.notes: Lecture only — lab is separate block

content_blocks.name: Ch 7: The Human Body (A&P)
content_blocks.notes: FULL DAY. 4 blocks of ~60 min. Longest chapter in course.

content_blocks.name: Ch 8: Pathophysiology
content_blocks.notes: Connects to Ch 7 — teach day after A&P

content_blocks.name: Ch 9: Life Span Development
content_blocks.notes: Can pair with Ch 8 same day

content_blocks.name: Ch 10: Patient Assessment
content_blocks.notes: Most important clinical chapter. 2 lecture blocks.

content_blocks.name: Ch 11: Airway Management
content_blocks.notes: 3 blocks of 60 min. Teach before airway lab.

content_blocks.name: Ch 13: Vascular Access & Med Admin
content_blocks.notes: Heavy on IV theory. Teach before IV lab.

content_blocks.name: Ch 15: BLS Resuscitation
content_blocks.notes: Shorter lecture — most students have CPR cert. Lab is separate.

content_blocks.name: Ch 17: Respiratory Emergencies
content_blocks.notes: 2 blocks (~90 + 80 min). Builds directly on Ch 11 airway.

content_blocks.name: Ch 28: Soft-Tissue Injuries
content_blocks.notes: Burns, bandaging. Can pair with Ch 27.

content_blocks.name: Ch 30: Head & Spine Injuries
content_blocks.notes: TBI, spinal cord injury — HIGH YIELD

content_blocks.name: Ch 32: Abdominal & GU Injuries
content_blocks.notes: Can pair with Ch 31 same day

content_blocks.name: Ch 35: OB & Neonatal Care
content_blocks.notes: 120 slides. Delivery, complications, NRP. Full day with lab.

content_blocks.name: Ch 38: Special Challenges
content_blocks.notes: Shorter chapter. Trach care, bariatric, developmental.

content_blocks.name: Ch 39: Transport Operations
content_blocks.notes: Condensed — academy grads know most of this

content_blocks.name: IV Access Lab Day 2
content_blocks.notes: Live sticks if policy allows. Consecutive day strongly preferred.

content_blocks.name: Legal/Ethics Case Discussion
content_blocks.notes: 4 scenarios. Full class discussion. Follows Ch 3 lecture.

content_blocks.name: A&P Worksheet
content_blocks.notes: Printed worksheet. Follows Ch 7 lecture blocks.

content_blocks.name: Assessment Scenario Practice
content_blocks.notes: 4 patient scenarios. Can be solo instructor with 30 students.

content_blocks.name: Vital Signs Practice
content_blocks.notes: Students pair up. Manual BP, pulse, RR, SpO2, pupils. 2 preferred.

content_blocks.name: Drug Card Activity
content_blocks.notes: Students create/fill in drug cards for 8 AEMT meds.

content_blocks.name: IV Math & Drip Rate Calculations
content_blocks.notes: Guided practice. Can be lecture-style with full class.

content_blocks.name: Bandaging & Burn Practice
content_blocks.notes: Pairs practice on each other. Low equipment.

content_blocks.name: MCI Triage Tabletop
content_blocks.notes: START triage exercise. Works with 30 students + 1 instructor.

content_blocks.name: NREMT Preparation Briefing
content_blocks.notes: Exam format, study plan, test-taking strategies.

content_blocks.name: 12-Lead Placement Practice
content_blocks.notes: Electrode placement on each other or manikin.

content_blocks.name: EMSTesting Group Testing
content_blocks.notes: 15-20 questions projected, class discusses. End-of-day reinforcement.

content_blocks.name: Comprehensive Final Exam
content_blocks.notes: ALL chapters. ~100-150 questions. Plan full morning.

content_blocks.name: Pharmacology Checkpoint
content_blocks.notes: Ch 12 must be taught. Difficulty level varies.

content_blocks.name: Day Closeout / Preview
content_blocks.notes: End of every day. Reading assignments, preview tomorrow.

default_template.name: LVFR AEMT 10-Week Standard
default_template.description: Standard 10-week LVFR AEMT course layout following ideal content sequence

ideal_sequence.title: Bleeding + Soft-Tissue + Stop the Bleed Lab
Pediatrics (Part 2) + Geriatric + Special Challenges + SPECIAL POP EXAM
EMS Ops (Ch 39-42) + EMS OPS EXAM + Final Review
